untranslatable_reason: This is a statistical analysis method (Z-score) for measuring trading streak significance, not a position sizing rule. It describes how to calculate confidence limits from historical data, but provides no actionable sizing parameters (no % of capital, no multipliers, no scaling thresholds). The formula (X*(X-N))/(N-1) appears to be a streak length calculation, not a position size directive. Without explicit guidance on how Z-score output translates to position size adjustments, this cannot be operationalized as a sizing method.
